BAYERN MATCH HUDSON-ODOI PRICE

Bayern Munich have tabled a fourth bid for Callum Hudson-Odoi, matching Chelsea’s required asking price of £35m, according to Sky sources.

Sky Sports News has been told Hudson-Odoi will not be taking up the latest contract offer from Chelsea - and sees his future at the Bundesliga champions.

We also understand that throughout a fortnight of negotiations, Chelsea have remained adamant that their asking price of £35m has to be met for the 18-year old, who has 18-months left on his current contract.

Bayern remain determined to land their number one target this window. Chelsea’s CEO Marina Granovskaia will now have to decide whether to accept Bayern’s latest bid.

HERRERA, LLORENTE ON BILBAO RADAR

Manchester United midfielder Ander Herrera and Tottenham striker Fernando Llorente are transfer targets for Athletic Bilbao, according to the club’s new director of football Rafa Alkorta.

In an interview with Spanish radio network Cadena SER, Alkorta said: "We are interested in Javi Martinez, Ibai Gomez, Ander Herrera, Fernando Llorente... all of them interest us."

As part of club policy, Athletic Bilbao only sign players born in the Basque Country or who came through the youth ranks of Basque clubs.

Both Herrera and Llorente meet that criteria and have already had previous spells at the club, playing 128 and 327 games for Bilbao respectively.

BALOTELLI HOPEFUL ON MARSEILLE

Mario Balotelli is still holding out hope of signing for Marseille, according to L'Equipe.

The 28-year-old former Manchester City and Liverpool forward is expected to leave Nice either this month or at the end of the season, when his contract expires. Although a potential return to Italy or England has been touted, Marseille seems a more probable destination.

The January transfer window is open and Victor Moses' Chelsea career is drawing to a close, with the 28-year-old having become a forgotten man at Stamford Bridge.

The versatile winger has only started one game all season and he hasn't featured at all since October.

Less than two years after playing a pivotal part in Chelsea's Premier League title triumph under Antonio Conte, Moses has been deemed surplus to requirements by Maurizio Sarri.

CARDIFF TARGET THREE

Cardiff City are targeting three signings from France before the end of the transfer window, according to Sky Sports News.

The trio are Nantes striker Emiliano Sala, Nice midfielder Adrien Tameze and a potential loan for Marseille winger Clinton N’Jie.

The Bluebirds have not given up on their pursuit of Sala - currently the third top-scorer in Ligue 1 with 12 goals – despite boss Neil Warnock admitting to doubts over whether a deal could be done last month.

It is understood Nantes are open to selling the 28-year-old and it is now down to Cardiff to convince him to move to South Wales.

AS Monaco in preliminary discussions to sign William Vainqueur

Our colleagues at Foot Mercato report that Ligue 1 side AS Monaco have had preliminary discussions with 30-year-old French midfielder William Vainqueur.

The former AS Roma and Marseille player, who was worked with Thierry Henry’s new assistant Franck Passi before, is currently contracted to Antalyaspor, but is surplus to requirements in the eyes of the Turkish side.

Talks are expected to accelerate for him imminently on Monaco’s part.

Schalke offer €9m + for Kevin Mbabu

Schalke are interested in signing BSC Young Boys defender Kevin Mbabu, according to Bild.

The Bundesliga club have already offered €9 million plus future incentives for the Switzerland international with Young Boys holding out for a fee closer to €13.3m.

With funds limited at Schalke, sporting director Christian Heidel will allow Franco Di Santo and Johannes Geis to leave the Bundesliga club.

A six-month loan deal with a purchase to buy in the summer could be a possible outcome for Mbabu, per Bild.

RMC report this morning that Bayern Munich have made formal contact with PSG over the possible signing of want-away midfielder Adrien Rabiot.

Despite the player being virtually in agreement with Barcelona over a deal that would see him join the Catalan giants for free in summer, Bayern Sporting Director Hasan Salihamidzic has been in discussions with Les Parisiens about buying the player for a small transfer fee this month.

SM Caen in advanced talks to loan Wolfsburg winger Paul-Georges Ntep

Cameroonian international winger Paul-Georges Ntep is in advanced negotiations with Ligue 1 strugglers Caen about a loan move until the end of the season from Wolfsburg, according to L’Équipe.

The 26-year-old attacker’s time in the Bundesliga has been torrid, playing just 15 times since his arrival in 2017.

SMC are not alone in their interest, with Nantes having enquired in the event of a potential Emiliano Sala departure and Guingamp have also made their interest known.

At the moment, Caen lead the race, a club that is willing to guarantee Ntep a starting berth.

Outside of France, Torino and Alavés have been in contact with Ntep’s entourage, but the player wants to return to the country where he made a name for himself at Rennes.

JOEL AND WILLOCK RETURN TO THE REDS

Two Manchester United players have returned to the fold after loan spells away from the club for the first part of the season.

The talented 22-year-old keeper, who enjoyed a senior Reds debut against Wigan Athletic in 2017, made 10 appearances in all competitions for Vitoria, during a second stint in the Primeira Liga after previously spending time with Belenenses.

Meanwhile, the paperwork has been completed for Matty Willock head back from Scotland after St Mirren activated the termination clause in his loan agreement.

The 22-year-old midfielder, whose brother Joe scored twice for Arsenal in their Emirates FA Cup win at Blackpool last weekend, appeared 14 times for the Buddies after a similar move to St Johnstone in 2018.

HOWE: WILSON NOT FOR SALE

Eddie Howe insists Callum Wilson is not for sale "at any price" as the Bournemouth striker continues to be linked to Chelsea.

“I’ve been told about the speculation, it’s not something I seek to read," he said.

"It’s a waste of time for me to do so. It’s business as normal, Callum has been bright and bubbly this week so no problems there.

"In my opinion, and it’s just my opinion, I wouldn’t sell at any price but I’m the manager of the team trying to create the best team that I can.

“We desperately want to take the team forward and to do that we have got to keep our best players.

“He (Wilson) is a very important member of the team. He has unique attributes that really complement what we do here.

“He’s been a really good goal scorer for us this season, that’s the hardest things to do in the sport so he value him really highly.”

Gabbiadini completes move to Sampdoria

Southampton Football Club can confirm that Manolo Gabbiadini has completed a permanent move to Sampdoria for an undisclosed fee.

The 27-year-old striker, who joined Saints in January 2017 from Napoli, now returns to Italy, signing for the seventh-placed Serie A side as they bid to secure a European place.

Gabbiadini scored 12 goals in 60 appearances during his time with Southampton, including a brace in the 2017 EFL Cup final, as well as the winner in the crucial away victory at Swansea last season, which effectively ensured the club’s Premier League status.

Lazio have been focusing on departures as of late, but now, they are turning to acquisitions. MORENO GETS HOMESICK

Alberto Moreno has told Liverpool he wants to return to Spain at the end of his contract this summer, according to The Sun.

The defender reportedly wants to join Sevilla, the club Liverpool signed him from in 2014.

Last month, he said: "I am in my fifth year here, there is no agreement on a renewal and in January I am free to listen to any offer."
Juventus are plotting to make a summer move for Real Madrid outcast Isco, who has a £630m release clause.

David Luiz will return to Benfica if he is not offered a new deal by Chelsea, according to the Daily Express and Daily Mirror.

The 31-year-old has started all but one league game this season, and has also reportedly been attracting interest from his come country of Brazil - with Palmeiras said to have enquired about him - but the defender is understood to be a fan of arch-rivals Corinthians.

Arsenal's reported bid to bring Yannick Carrasco to the Emirates could be hit by the Spaniard's wage demands, according to the Daily Star.

Unai Emery is said to be keen to reunite with the left winger, who he worked with at Sevilla, but has his hands tied by Arsenal's tight finances in the January window.

Yesterday the Gunners boss said: "We cannot sign permanently. We can only loan players. Only loan players. I can say to you that the club is working, the club is telling me the different situations in each moment. At the moment, we haven't had any news today about that."

Chelsea target Gonzalo Higuain wants to leave Milan, says Gennaro Gattuso

AC Milan head coach Gennaro Gattuso has confirmed Chelsea target Gonzalo Higuain wants to leave the club.

"When a player makes up his mind it becomes hard to convince them otherwise," said Gattuso of Higuain.

"I have a relationship with him that features great honesty, we say things to one another's face.

"At the moment, he's our player and we're holding on tightly to him. I don't know what will happen."

"I've talked to him a great deal but it's hard to give advice because the career of a player only lasts 13-14 years," continued Gattuso.

"It's his mind, not mine. The most important thing is to talk as men, look each other in the eyes and speak the truth.

"I still don't understand what Higuain's unhappy about because I see a happy lad, involved in the locker room.

"We'll see what happens. If it was up to me, I'd keep him at my house and feed him my dinner."

https://www.skysports.com/football/news/11863/11606164/chelsea-target-gonzalo-higuain-wants-to-leave-milan-says-gennaro-gattuso

Michael Zorc denies Shinji Kagawa departure

BeIN reported this morning that Ligue 1 side had agreed a deal to bring Borussia Dortmund midfielder Shinji Kagawa to France, who reportedly isn’t convinced on a transfer.

It has since been reported by Ruhr Nachrichten, who has close ties to BVB, that sporting director Michael Zorc knows nothing of said loan, telling the outlet: “Of this, I know nothing.”

Kicker had previously reported that the Japan international would prefer a move to Spain should the opportunity arise.

https://www.getfootballnewsgermany.com/2019/michael-zorc-denies-shinji-kagawa-departure/

DOUCOURE: I AM GOING TO LEAVE

Some strong and infortmative quotes to start the day from Watford midfielder Abdoulaye Doucoure, who has been linked with a move to PSG.

Speaking to French TV station Canal Plus: “I think I am going to leave Watford. The club know my ambitions and want to help me get to the next level.

“I’m flattered by the interest of PSG but for the moment there’s nothing concrete. I will focus on my performance for my club.

“I’ve not had contact with PSG and they’ve not contacted Watford. My goal is to play well for Watford and then maybe join a Champions League team.

“The chances of me joining PSG? Probably small as they follow a number of players. I’m not the priority that’s for sure but if there is an opportunity then we will look into it....This winter we’ll study all possibilities on offer. The most important thing for me is to continue to progress and with my performances I’ll succeed in playing for a club in Europe.

“Staying in England is a possibility and the Italian league is becoming more interesting. There are clubs who have returned to show interest in me again. The first criteria for me is that the club plays in the Champions League.”

Conor Coady in line for new Wolves contract with defender also on Gareth Southgate’s England radar

Wolverhampton Wanderers are poised to open contract talks with captain Conor Coady, who is on Gareth Southgate’s radar for an England call-up.

Coady is in line for a new deal after making a huge impression in the Premier League under Nuno Espirito Santo.

The defender’s wages were bumped up to around £36,000 a week after promotion from the Championship but Wolves are ready to start negotiations over improved terms later this month.

https://www.telegraph.co.uk/football/2019/01/13/conor-coady-line-new-wolves-contract-defender-also-gareth-southgates/

JOEL AND WILLOCK RETURN TO THE REDS

Two Manchester United players have returned to the fold after loan spells away from the club for the first part of the season.

United can confirm we have activated the recall clause in Joel Pereira's loan agreement with Vitoria Setubal, ending his temporary spell in Portugal.

The talented 22-year-old keeper, who enjoyed a senior Reds debut against Wigan Athletic in 2017, made 10 appearances in all competitions for Vitoria, during a second stint in the Primeira Liga after previously spending time with Belenenses.

Meanwhile, the paperwork has been completed for Matty Willock head back from Scotland after St Mirren activated the termination clause in his loan agreement.

The 22-year-old midfielder, whose brother Joe scored twice for Arsenal in their Emirates FA Cup win at Blackpool last weekend, appeared 14 times for the Buddies after a similar move to St Johnstone in 2018.

OKAZAKI WANTS OUT

Shinji Okazaki wants to leave Leicester City in the hope of getting more football, but Leicester are reluctant to let him go because they don’t feel they have enough cover up front, Sky Sports News understands.

A number of Premier League clubs are interested in signing him.

The Japanese international was left out of Leicester’s matchday squad for Saturday’s defeat to Southampton - the first time that’s happened for 14 matches.

And Okazaki told Japanese media after the game:

“I can't feel satisfied as a player. After three-and-a-half years, maybe it's time to move on. I have no intention of quarrelling with the team, but I will make my feelings known.”

WEST HAM JOIN WILSON CHASE

West Ham will target Bournemouth striker Callum Wilson if they decide to let Marko Arnautovic leave for China, according to Sky sources.

Shanghai SIPG want to sign Arnautovic, but had a £35m offer for the Austria international rejected by West Ham last week.

Arnautovic’s agent and brother, Danijel, revealed last week that the West Ham striker wanted to leave and called on the club to accept the offer from the Chinese Super League side.

Manager Manuel Pellegrini will have the final say on whether West Ham sell Arnautovic, who is valued at over £50m.

Leonardo Balerdi to sign five-year deal with Borussia Dortmund

Boca Juniors defender Leonardo Balerdi is expected to sign a five-year contract with Borussia Dortmund on Monday, according to Bild.

The two clubs agreed on a deal worth around €15 million while it was reported on Friday that the Argentina U20 international is expected in Dortmund this week to undergo his medical.

Balerdi (19) will then return to Chile on Wednesday to play for Argentina in the U20 South American Championship although BVB are trying to keep hold of the youngster with injuries to Manuel Akanji, Dan-Axel Zagadou and Abdou Diallo.

https://www.getfootballnewsgermany.com/2019/leonardo-balerdi-to-sign-five-year-deal-with-borussia-dortmund/

France Football report that Real Betis striker Antonio Sanabria has been offered to struggling Marseille.

Sanabria scored 8 goals in 17 appearances last season, but is not being used much at all by Betis in 2018/19 – a loan deal with an option to buy could be a possibility.

Wolverhampton Wanderers are close to agreeing a loan deal for Leo Bonatini to join Greek side PAOK for the remainder of this season, Sky Sports News understands.

The forward didn’t score a league goal for Wolves in the whole of the 2018 calendar year, he hasn’t started a Premier League game for Wolves this season, and has not played under Nuno Espirito Santo at all, since 3 November.

Both Wolves and the player think it would be beneficial for him to get some game time elsewhere. After failing to persuade Tammy Abraham to switch Villa Park for Molineux, signing a striker in January is still Wolves’ top priority this window.

Juventus could retain Spinazzola as Darmian and Emerson options have stalled


Juventus are discussing a deal with Bologna that would send Leonardo Spinazzola to the Emilian club. While it appeared at first as if a deal could be close, but now it appears that Spinazzola could stay in Turin.

Juve had other options for alternatives in mind, first Matteo Darmian, who just had his contract 2020 extension exercised by Man United. Also, Emerson Palmieri, who the bianconeri have followed since before his move to Chelsea when he was at Roma. The fullback came up during discussions with Chelsea during negotations over Higuain.

https://gianlucadimarzio.com/en/juventus-could-retain-spinazzola-as-darmian-and-emerson-options-have-stalled

Napoli serious about Celta Vigo's Lobotka: the latest


Napoli are not giving up on their objective for midfield. They are negotiating for Celta Vigo's Lobotka. The Azzurri have revived contacts for the Slovakian, who was also a target last summer. There was a phone call between Ancelotti and it was well-received by the player. Napoli are offering 2 million euros for the loan and another 18 million euros for the option to buy, but Celta are asking for more. The feeling is that the deal could be concluded at 5 million euros for the loan and 20 million euros for the option to buy. Napoli are instituting to get a "yes" from Lobotka, but they have to anticipate the competion from Bournemouth and formulate the right offer for the Spanish club.

https://gianlucadimarzio.com/en/napoli-serious-about-celta-vigos-lobotka-the-latest

Lazio's Caceres an option for Bologna's defense: the situation


Bologna are consider options for their defense, the idea of Spinazzola from Juventus has not progressed, so they will turn to other options. Lazio's Martin Caceres is not a part of the current project, and so was linked to a move to Parma and now Bologna will look to make the player an offer. There have been positive initial talks between the player and club. While Spinazzola does not seem like an option currently, Bologna have an alternative in Chievo's Fabrzio Cacciatore.

https://gianlucadimarzio.com/en/lazios-caceres-an-option-for-bolognas-defense-the-situation

Man Utd players want Ole Gunnar Solskjaer to get full-time job as Paul Pogba hails interim manager's impact

Paul Pogba says Ole Gunnar Solskjaer has put a “smile” back on his face after emerging from “the shadows” under Jose Mourinho to spearhead Manchester United’s resurgence this season.

Pogba’s praise for Solskjaer was echoed by team-mates Jesse Lingard and Ander Herrera in the wake of Sunday’s 1-0 win over Tottenham at Wembley and The Telegraph understands there is growing momentum within the Old Trafford dressing room for the interim manager to be handed the job on a permanent basis at the end of the season.

Update on Higuain’s future

The future of Gonzalo Higuain remains up in the air.

The Argentine striker has genuinely thought about leaving the Rossoneri during the current season, according to Romeo Agresti of Goal Italia.

Pipita did not take sporting director Leonardo’s recent comments too well while he also realizes his future at AC Milan is directly linked to qualifying for the Champions League.

Meanwhile, there is his former coach Maurizio Sarri waiting in the background.

Chelsea’s main doubt is Higuain’s age as owner Roman Abramovich prefers investing in younger players.

As Juventus still own the 31-year-old striker, they are looking for guarantees in the short-term and long-term.

Higuain joined Milan this past summer on a €18 million loan deal with an option to buy for €36 million but the end of the story is still unknown.

Milan have already begun to explore options in case Higuain departs, notably Genoa striker Piatek.

Meanwhile, Juventus could be open to Higuain joining Chelsea on an 18-month loan as the Bianconeri await a call from London.

https://www.getfootballnewsitaly.com/2019/update-on-higuain-future/

Aboubakar Kamara fought Aleksandr Mitrovic last week during a yoga session

French striker Aboubakar Kamara was involved in a physical fight with Fulham team-mate Aleksandr Mitrovic last week during a team yoga, relaxation session, according to Charlie Wyett.

The incident occurred last week, less than a month after penalty-taking fiasco when Kamara refused to allow Mitrovic to take penalty earned against Huddersfield only for the Amiens man to miss the subsequent spot-kick.

Mitrovic and Kamara’s yoga row started when the Serbian international confronted the Frenchman for apparently being rude to the yoga instructor – the pair were separated by team-mates after physically coming to blows with each other.

Claudio Ranieri, who is understood to have been shocked by both incidents, is reportedly ready to sell Kamara the 23-year-old this window, and views the Frenchman as a perennial troublemaker.

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/aboubakar-kamara-fought-aleksandr-mitrovic-last-week-during-a-yoga-session/

Aboubakar Kamara fought Aleksandr Mitrovic last week during a yoga session

French striker Aboubakar Kamara was involved in a physical fight with Fulham team-mate Aleksandr Mitrovic last week during a team yoga, relaxation session, according to Charlie Wyett.

The incident occurred last week, less than a month after penalty-taking fiasco when Kamara refused to allow Mitrovic to take penalty earned against Huddersfield only for the Amiens man to miss the subsequent spot-kick.

Mitrovic and Kamara’s yoga row started when the Serbian international confronted the Frenchman for apparently being rude to the yoga instructor – the pair were separated by team-mates after physically coming to blows with each other.

Claudio Ranieri, who is understood to have been shocked by both incidents, is reportedly ready to sell Kamara the 23-year-old this window, and views the Frenchman as a perennial troublemaker.

Michy Batshuayi wants AS Monaco move, but offers continue to rain in

Belgian international striker Michy Batshuayi has informed Chelsea representatives of his desire to move to AS Monaco on loan until the end of the season, but the Blues have been hit with such a large volume of offers for the former Marseille man that they are delaying giving the all clear, according to RMC.

Bids from Fiorentina (loan, no option to buy) and Napoli (loan, with option to buy) as well as a persistence on Premier League club Everton’s part to attempt to purchase Michy outright has given Chelsea food for thought, even though the player is set on reuniting with Thierry Henry following their time together in the Belgium national team.

The Belgian had been expected to sign no later than yesterday with Monaco, and the deal is entirely prepped barring the authorisation of the FIFA TMS system, but it is clear that other bids have slowed down a deal.

RMC remain confident that Batshuayi will join Monaco on loan until the end of the season.

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/michy-batshuayi-wants-as-monaco-move-but-offers-continue-to-rain-in/

Milan, meeting with Zapata’s agent to discuss extension

A rash of injuries at the center back position gave an opportunity for significant playing time to Cristian Zapata who made the most of the predicament. In fact after Rino Gattuso’s complimentary words towards him, it’s very likely that the Colombian defender will sign an extension.

Today Zapata’s agent Ivan Ramiro Córdoba attended a meeting at Casa Milan to discuss a new deal since his current contract expires at the end of the season. The summit lasted fourth five minutes with Córdoba leaving Casa Milan ar 14:50 local time.

https://gianlucadimarzio.com/en/milan-meeting-with-zapatas-agent-to-discuss-extension

Edinson Cavani: “I have 1.5 years left on my contract… I am nearly 32. I think about going back to my country.”

Speaking in an interview with RMC, 31-year-old Uruguayan marksman Edinson Cavani discussed his future and contract situation with Paris Saint-Germain.

“We have not discussed my contract. I am very happy in Paris. I feel happy, with my family. The affection that the fans show me motivates me, gives me strength. If at some point, we are to talk about a contract, then we will talk about a contract. We will see what the club’s aims are.”

“I have 1.5 years left on my deal. If I am given the possibility to stay in Paris, I will stay. The club has shown a lot of faith in me. I do not know if I will finish my contract and go back to my country (Uruguay). The truth is that this is also something that I think about. I am nearly 32. To go back to my country, be with my family, my loved ones, I think about it. For 13 years now I have been far away, in Europe.”

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/edinson-cavani-i-have-1-5-years-left-on-my-contract-i-am-nearly-32-i-think-about-going-back-to-my-country/

Emiliano Sala met with Neil Warnock in Cardiff yesterday, but remains sceptical of moving

L’Équipe report this morning, as other English outlets have affirmed, that Nantes striker Emiliano Sala met with Cardiff City boss Neil Warnock yesterday.

The French newspaper claims that for 4 weeks already, Nantes and Cardiff have had a £20m agreement for the player’s transfer in place. However, the 28-year-old is not keen on leaving Les Canaris.

If he were to leave this month, he wants it to be for a club that is playing European football.

Nantes manager Vahid Halilhodzic has made it publicly clear on multiple occasions that he wants the club to offer Sala a contract extension, with the striker’s current deal expiring in 2020. But such an offer has never been made. Why? The Nantes board actively wants to sell Sala, and have repeated as much to the player in question in recent days.

Because of President Kita’s desire to see Sala leave, the Argentinian agreed to visit Cardiff on Monday to meet with manager Neil Warnock.

Was it an instant hit? No – as of late last night, Sala was yet to make a decision.

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/emiliano-sala-met-with-neil-warnock-in-cardiff-yesterday-but-remains-sceptical-of-moving/

"We have to find a good mix between young talents and experienced leadership players."

David De Gea is ready to commit his future to Manchester United, reports The Sun.

The Spain international is under contract at Old Trafford until 2020 after United triggered a one-year extension in his contract in November.

Securing the goalkeeper’s long-term future has remained a priority since, and The Sun claim United’s hopes extending De Gea’s stay have been boosted by the sacking of Jose Mourinho.

It is understood De Gea believes his trophy hopes have been boosted since the sacking of the Portuguese.

Chelsea are one of a number of Premier League clubs due in Holland this weekend to monitor the progress of Heerenveen’s highly-rated left winger Arber Zeneli, Sky Sports News has been told.

The 23-year old Kosovo international is out of contract in the summer and could be available for as little as £5m.

Before representing Kosovo, Zeneli played for Sweden at U17, U19 and U21 level.

Arsenal set to clinch Suarez in '€2+20m' deal

Arsenal are on the verge of sealing Denis Suarez's arrival from Barcelona after meeting the Catalan club's demands over the midfielder, reports RAC 1 (via Mundo Deportivo).

Under the terms of the prospective deal, the Gunners would pay out €2 million in an initial loan move and a further €20m (£17.5m/$22.8m) in the summer to make the transfer permanent.

https://www.mundodeportivo.com/futbol/fc-barcelona/20190117/454186987554/barca-denis-suarez-millones-arsenal-cesion-fichaje.html

Sky sources understand Cardiff City have agreed a deal to sign Emiliano Sala but it is being held up because Nantes want him to play one final game for them - against Angers on Sunday.

Cardiff want to complete the deal in time for Sala to play at Newcastle on Saturday.

The Argentinian striker has agreed personal terms and is waiting for permission to fly to Cardiff for a medical.

The fee is believed to be in the region of £18m.

Shanghai SIPG want to sign Dimitri Payet from Marseille, according to Sky sources.

The Chinese side have had a £35m offer for Marko Arnautovic rejected by West Ham.

They are now also exploring the possibility of signing former West Ham fans’ favourite Payet.

Payet left West Ham in acrimonious circumstances for Marseille two years ago in a £25m deal. He is 32 in March and Shanghai SIPG are prepared to offer him a contract which would more than double his wages to over £200,000 a week.

Payet has scored 4 times in 18 league appearances this season. Marseille are ninth in Ligue 1.

PSG attacker Jesé Rodriguez rejects an approach from Valladolid

RMC report that Spanish attacker Jesé will not be returning to Paris after partaking in the Qatar Winter Tour with the rest of his team-mates, instead he will fly to Spain.

The reason behind that is to spend time with his family, which PSG manager Thomas Tuchel has signed off on.

Jesé has in the meantime rejected a proposal from La Liga side Real Valladolid, with 13 days of the January window remaining.

The Spaniard, who has 2.5 years remaining on his current contract, would prefer a return to his native country, on loan.

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/psg-attacker-jese-rodriguez-rejects-an-approach-from-valladolid/

Watford are holding up PSG’s January transfer window

France Football report that PSG have failed to advance in their pursuit of a central midfielder in recent days because of Watford’s activity in the transfer market.

Everton want Abdoulaye Doucouré to replace Idrissa Gueye, who the Toffees have agreed in principle to sell to PSG, if they find a replacement, but the Hornets are not allowing the Frenchman to join EFC for anything less than €50m, which Marco Silva’s club consider unreasonable.

As a result, Les Parisiens’ number one priority is Julian Weigl of Borussia Dortmund, who is also the chief option for PSG boss Thomas Tuchel.

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/watford-are-holding-up-psgs-january-transfer-window/

PSG restart talks for Stanislav Lobotka

PSG, remain in active discussions for Abdoulaye Doucouré (Watford), Julian Weigl (Dortmund), Leandro Paredes (Zenit) and Idrissa Gueye (Everton), fear that none of those negotiations will bare fruit and as a result have restarted talks for Celta Vigo midfielder Stanislav Lobotka, according to Paris United.

The player has a €50m release clause, but an offer of €30m should be enough to convince the Spanish side, according to the outlet in question.

As it stands, he is looked upon as a secondary option. Napoli have made a loan with option to buy offer for the player, whilst Bournemouth, Wolves and Watford are all interested.

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/psg-restart-talks-for-stanislav-lobotka/

Shanghai SIPG are courting Dimitri Payet, who isn’t interested

Chinese Super League side Shanghai SIPG are actively courting Marseille winger and captain Dimitri Payet, as first reported by Sky Sports.

The Chinese side would double Payet’s wages to over £200k a week, but at the time of writing have yet make a formal offer to Ligue 1 side OM.

La Provence report that the player is not interested in the move, viewing the idea of leaving Marseille in a period of crisis as something he wouldn’t do. He intends to stay and see OM turn their form around.

Nabil Fékir offers update on his future

Speaking at length in an interview with Le Progrès, Lyon captain Nabil Fékir discussed his future at the club.

On a contract extension (current deal expires in 2020):

Discussions are ongoing. I have sat down with the president, things are advancing slowly. When will I take a decision? I am not fixing myself a date, I am not in a rush. Nobody is putting any pressure on it and I like to take my time.

On whether this is his final season at Lyon:

I do not know. Last year was supposed to be my last, and in the end I’m still here. I cannot know the answer to this. I am happy at Lyon, but which player doesn’t dream of discovering a new league, another club?

On Chelsea:

They haven’t called me, but they can be watching my situation without calling me (laughs).

Borussia Dortmund’s Michael Zorc: “Julian Weigl will stay in Dortmund.”

Borussia Dortmund sporting director Michael Zorc confirmed on Saturday night that Julian Weigl will remain at the club this window despite heavy interest from Ligue 1 outfit Paris Saint-Germain.

Speaking to broadcaster ZDF, Zorc said: “Julian Weigl will stay in Dortmund. He has already played very well against Gladbach [as centre-back] before the winter break and once again showed a brilliant performance tonight, especially when keeping in mind that he usually plays in midfield.”

PSG remain in active discussions to sign the Germany international but Borussia Dortmund’s stance remains the same, Weigl will not be leaving the Bundesliga leaders just yet.

Dawid Kownacki expected to join Fortuna Düsseldorf imminently

Fortuna Düsseldorf will complete the loan signing of Sampdoria forward Dawid Kownacki early next week, according to Kicker.

The Poland international will join the Bundesliga club on an initial six-month loan deal with a €7 million purchase option.

Sampdoria Coach Marco Giampaolo has already confirmed that Kownacki will be leaving the Serie A club while the 21-year-old failed to make the squad for Sunday’s fixture against Fiorentina, suggesting a move to Germany will be imminent.

Eintracht Frankfurt interested in signing Monaco defender Almamy Touré

Bundesliga side Eintracht Frankfurt are interested in signing 22-year-old Franco-Malian defender Almamy Touré from AS Monaco, according to our colleagues at Goal France.

Touré is out of contract this summer and has entered into discussions with Frankfurt about signing on a free transfer at the end of the season.

He has only played 5 matches in all competitions all campaign so far and a move to the Bundesliga with Frankfurt would seem him join up with former Ligue 1 and Ligue 2 players Evan Ndicka, Kevin Trapp, Simon Falette, Gelson Fernandes and Sébastien Haller.

Touré has rebuffed contract extension offers from Monaco to date.

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/eintracht-frankfurt-interested-in-signing-monaco-defender-almamy-toure/

CELTA IN HOEDT TALKS

Celta Vigo are in talks with Southampton over a deal to sign centre-back Wesley Hoedt, Sky Sports News understands.

The two clubs are in discussions over an initial loan deal with a view to buying the 24-year-old in the summer.

New Saints boss Ralph Hasenhuttel recently admitted Hoedt was free to leave St Mary’s this month, having not featured since he succeeded Mark Hughes in December.

SSN reported earlier this month that Celta had been in talks with Crystal Palace over a similar deal for their defender Jairo Riedewald.

Sassuolo in search of Boateng replacement: Balotelli is an option

With the news of the move today for Kevin-Prince Boateng to Barcelona, Sassuolo are active looking for his replacement. There are two options that Sassuolo are looking at. First is Sampdoria’s Gianluca Caprari. Caprari is not currently available, though the winger is the first choice for De Zerbi.

The other name is Balotelli, who has not completed the final details with a move to Marseille (there have been steps taken but currently no completed deal). Balotelli has been offered to Sassuolo, and they are considering whether to make an offer.

PSG continuing their push for Napoli’s Allan

Paris Saint-Germain are again asking for Allan, Napoli’s Brazilian midfielder. PSG’s love of the player is well known, and there is interest as well from Allan to move to new surroundings. But there has not been a concrete offer made yet: PSG are thought to value the player at around €50-60 million plus bonuses, while Napoli wish for €120 million. There is distance between the clubs, but there is still daylight for finding an agreement. PSG meanwhile continue pressing for Paredes from Zenit.

https://gianlucadimarzio.com/en/psg-continuing-their-push-for-napolis-allan

Genoa, new talks for Pjaca: Juventus says yes

As a decisive meeting is scheduled between Genoa and Milan tomorrow for the transfer of Piatek, the rossoblu also continue to work on signings. They have made new contacts for Marko Pjaca.

Preziosi has the ‘yes’ from Juventus (who know that he will play more than he has for Fiorentina) and now hopes to get the yes from Pjaca himself for a transfer. They are working towards a resolution and are looking for an agreement between all parties, and could possibly even close the operation in the coming days. Genoa is insisting on signing the player and would like to quickly add him to Prandelli’s squad.

https://gianlucadimarzio.com/en/genoa-pjaca

Ligue 1 strugglers AS Monaco sounded out Manchester City’s French central defender Eliaquim Mangala earlier on during this window before moving to other targets, according to France Football.

Thierry Henry’s club decided to look at other options owing to concerns that the ASM board had about Mangala’s form and fitness, with the 27-year-old having only played 3 matches – for the Manchester City reserve team – with no other competitive action, all season so far.

Eden Hazard asked if his career choices will be determined by Zinedine
Zidane’s next move: “No… I am not going to Manchester.”

Speaking in an interview with France Football, Chelsea attacker Eden Hazard was naturally asked about his future with the London club – see the exchange as it occurred below.

Do you want to see something else?

Yes. And I have always said it. That after England, I want to discover something else. But there are things that mean I could also stay.

Do you feel like you are at a turning point in your career?

I am not far from the summit of my career, that is for sure. Even if it could always be better, that is also clear. I am at a turning point yes. We will take the right route.

Do you need a new environment to be even better?

No. Look at this season. After the World Cup, I intended to leave. In the end, I stayed with Chelsea and I am having one of my best ever seasons. So I do not think that I need something different. But that does not mean that I will be better.

Does Real Madrid interest you?

Why not?

Why not?

You know very well. You are asking me the question, but you already know the answer very well. So you don’t need to ask me the question. (laughs)

Real Madrid without Zinedine Zidane, is still Real Madrid no?

It is different. But yes, it remains Real Madrid.

Have you heard from him?

No. I have never spoken with him.

Are you paying attention to which destination he may take next?

No. My career will not be dependent on Zidane. If tomorrow, he goes to Manchester (United), I am not going to go to Manchester (United). I am not going to wait for him to take up with a new club to follow him. I think that he likes me. Me, we know all the love that I had for him as a played. But I am not waiting for him to sign somewhere so that I can just follow him, not at all.

https://www.getfootballnewsfrance.com/2019/eden-hazard-asked-if-his-career-choices-will-be-determined-by-zinedine-zidanes-next-move-no-i-am-not-going-to-manchester/

Usain Bolt signals professional football dream is over: 'It was fun while it lasted'

Jamaican sprint great Usain Bolt has signalled that his hopes of a professional football career are over, saying: "It was fun while it lasted."

Bolt has had several stints in training and on trial at clubs around the world. Most notably, he scored twice in a friendly match for Central Coast Mariners but was unable to agree a contract with the Australian club.

"I don't want to say it wasn't dealt with properly, but I think we went about it, not the way we should and you learn your lesson, you live and you learn," he said.

"It was a good experience. I really enjoyed just being in a team and it was different from track and field and it was fun while it lasted."

http://global.espn.com/soccer/soccer-transfers/story/3756488/usain-bolt-signals-professional-football-dream-is-over-it-was-fun-while-it-lasted

Stuttgart rebuff Bayern Munich’s attempts to lure Benjamin Pavard before summer

Following reports suggesting that Benjamin Pavard will leave VfB Stuttgart for Bayern Munich this month, VfB sporting director Michael Reschke has since rebuffed these claims.

“Pavard will stay with us. A premature move to Munich is not a topic at all,” Reschke told AZ.

L’Equipé today reported that Bayern Munich have already seen their €40 million – a further €5m than originally agreed – offer to bring the Frenchman to the Allianz Arena early declined.

It’s since been suggested that Bayern Munich will go back to Stuttgart with a further €10m on top of their original €35m agreement to bring Pavard to Munich in January.
